steps.title: Spot the structure
steps.content: The blank falls between the noun “analysis” and the phrase “on historical context...might yield.” Since “might yield” functions as the sentence's main verb, the blank must provide a modifier of “analysis,” not another main verb.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
Using the participle “focusing” forms a participial phrase that modifies “analysis” while “might yield” remains the sole main verb.

This conforms to the rule that participles modifying nouns must be non-finite.

The other options—would focus, focuses, and would be focusing—are all finite verbs that would create two main verbs in one clause, violating this rule.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
